overviewShort: Spanning 30 square miles along Long Island Sound, Fairfield anchors Connecticut's Gold Coast with three Metro-North stations, two universities, and a downtown Post Road corridor lined with boutiques like Brick Walk and restaurants like The Sinclair. The town's ten distinct neighborhoods range from the historic estates of Greenfield Hill to the coastal bungalows and modern builds of Fairfield Beach.

children.text: Three Metro-North stations stitch Fairfield into the New Haven Line: Fairfield Center, Fairfield Metro, and Southport. Express trains reach Grand Central Terminal in roughly 75 to 80 minutes; local service runs closer to 90. That triple-station coverage is rare on the Gold Coast and means most residents live within a five-minute drive of a platform. I-95 and the Merritt Parkway bracket the town north-south, putting Stamford 25 minutes away and midtown Manhattan under 90 minutes by car outside rush hour.

Downtown clusters along the Post Road between Beach Road and Ruane Street. Brick Walk anchors the commercial strip with brick-lined walkways, outdoor seating, and a curated mix of independent boutiques and national retailers. The Sinclair serves American cuisine on an open-air terrace overlooking the plaza. Molto brings Roman-inspired wine bar dining to the block. Fairfield Cheese Company stocks local and international selections alongside charcuterie and wine. The atmosphere is polished but not precious — a college-town energy from Fairfield University and Sacred Heart University keeps the energy up year-round.

Ten neighborhoods give Fairfield uncommon range. Southport, a designated historic district, preserves 18th- and 19th-century Federal and Greek Revival homes along a harbor anchored by the Pequot Yacht Club — average sale prices there topped $1 million in 2024. Greenfield Hill sits on rolling, tree-lined roads where the Greenfield Hill Congregational Church has hosted its Dogwood Festival since 1936; Eleanor Roosevelt visited specifically to view the blooms in 1938. Stratfield offers 1920s-1940s colonials near Owen Fish Park. Fairfield Beach is the rare Gold Coast address where residents walk to sand, downtown shops, and a train platform.

Schools drive demand. Fairfield Warde High School ranks 11th in the state with an A+ Niche rating. Fairfield Ludlowe High School places in the top 10% of all Connecticut schools, posting a 95% graduation rate. On the private side, Fairfield College Preparatory School — a Jesuit institution on the Fairfield University campus — enrolls roughly 800 students and offers 17 AP courses. Both Fairfield University (Jesuit, approximately 5,400 undergraduates) and Sacred Heart University (over 7,000 undergraduates on a 350-acre campus) contribute Division I athletics, performing arts, and a year-round calendar of public events.

The waterfront defines weekend life. Penfield Beach offers pavilion facilities, volleyball, and the Jessica Rekos Memorial Playground. Jennings Beach stretches across 27 acres with lifeguard service, concessions, and sailboat racks — dogs and horses are permitted October through March. Lake Mohegan, four miles inland, provides freshwater swimming, a sprinkler park, and hiking trails. In total, Fairfield maintains 170 acres of parkland and five miles of coastline. Add two golf courses, a dozen marinas, and the springtime Dogwood Festival in Greenfield Hill, and the town sustains an outdoor calendar that rivals communities twice its size.

lifestyle:
Three Metro-North stations — Fairfield Center, Fairfield Metro, and Southport — place nearly every neighborhood within a short drive of the New Haven Line. Express trains reach Grand Central Terminal in approximately 75 to 80 minutes; local service runs around 90. I-95 and the Merritt Parkway provide parallel highway access. Stamford is 25 minutes south, Hartford about an hour northeast.

The dining and retail corridor along Post Road has matured into a genuine downtown. Brick Walk shopping center mixes independent boutiques with restaurants around a landscaped, walkable courtyard. The Sinclair anchors the scene with American cuisine and an open terrace. Molto serves Roman-inspired small plates and natural wine. Geronimo brings Southwest-influenced cooking and one of the area's deeper tequila lists. Fairfield Cheese Company has become a destination for charcuterie, provisions, and weekend grazing boards. The university presence — Fairfield University and Sacred Heart — adds cultural programming, Division I athletics, and a dining and nightlife scene that other Gold Coast towns simply lack.

Weekends split between the coastline and the interior. Jennings Beach is the flagship: 27 acres of sand, volleyball courts, sailing racks, and summer lifeguard service. Penfield Beach has its pavilions and the Rekos Memorial Playground. Lake Mohegan offers freshwater swimming, a sprinkler park, and wooded trails four miles from the shore. Greenfield Hill's Dogwood Festival, running since 1936, draws thousands each May. The town maintains 170 acres of parkland, multiple marinas, and two golf courses. There is no off-season here.
marketNotes:
Fairfield's median sale price reached approximately $800,000 in late 2025, with the broader single-family market showing year-over-year gains near 6%. That positions the town above the Fairfield County median of $675,000 but well below neighboring Westport, which crossed $2 million. The spread creates a meaningful value proposition — Gold Coast schools, beaches, and commute access without the seven-figure floor of towns to the west.

Neighborhood-level pricing varies dramatically. Greenfield Hill averaged over $1.6 million through 2024. Southport cleared $1 million. Beach-area homes, especially modern new construction in Craftsman and Nantucket styles, have pushed into the $1.5 to $2 million range. Stratfield and Fairfield Woods offer the most accessible entry, with renovated colonials and capes available under $700,000 — increasingly rare on this stretch of coastline.

Inventory remains tight. Fairfield County closed 2025 with roughly 65% fewer active listings than 2019, and Fairfield proper mirrors that compression. Homes averaged around 30 days on market before going under contract, down nearly 17% year over year. Well-priced listings in the $600,000 to $1 million band still attract multiple offers. Above $1.5 million, buyers have more room to negotiate, but turnover in premium neighborhoods is low. Sellers who price accurately move quickly; those who reach sit.
highlights: Three Metro-North stations on the New Haven Line — Fairfield Center, Fairfield Metro, and Southport — with express service to Grand Central in 75 to 80 minutes
Fairfield Warde High School ranks 11th in Connecticut; Fairfield Ludlowe places in the state's top 10% with a 95% graduation rate
Jennings Beach spans 27 acres of waterfront with summer lifeguard service, volleyball, and sailing racks along Long Island Sound
Two universities — Fairfield University and Sacred Heart University — contribute Division I athletics, performing arts, and year-round public events
Greenfield Hill's annual Dogwood Festival, running since 1936, celebrates one of Connecticut's most iconic spring landscapes
Downtown Post Road and Brick Walk offer boutique shopping, artisan food, and upscale dining in a walkable courtyard setting
Ten distinct neighborhoods span from Southport's historic harbor district to Fairfield Beach's walkable coastal village
seoTitle: Fairfield CT Real Estate | Gold Coast Homes & Beaches
seoDescription: Discover Fairfield, Connecticut homes from coastal neighborhoods to historic estates. Schools, commute data, and market trends. Contact Matt Caiola today.

faqs.question: How long is the Metro-North commute from Fairfield to Grand Central Terminal?
faqs.answer: Fairfield has three Metro-North stations on the New Haven Line: Fairfield Center, Fairfield Metro, and Southport. Express trains reach Grand Central Terminal in approximately 75 to 80 minutes. Local service runs closer to 90 minutes. Trains depart roughly every 30 minutes during peak commuting hours, and monthly passes cost between $300 and $400 depending on the fare zone. Most residents are within a five-minute drive of at least one station.

faqs.question: What are the main neighborhoods in Fairfield, Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Fairfield has ten primary neighborhoods: Southport (historic harbor district with Federal and Greek Revival homes), Greenfield Hill (affluent, wooded lots with the famed Dogwood Festival), Fairfield Beach (coastal living within walking distance of sand and downtown), Stratfield (1920s-1940s colonials near Owen Fish Park), Fairfield Center (close to the train station and Post Road shops), Brooklawn (established residential area), Fairfield Woods (affordable entry point), Tunxis Hill (hillside properties), Grasmere (family-oriented), and the University area (near Fairfield University and Sacred Heart). Pricing ranges from under $700,000 in Stratfield to over $1.6 million in Greenfield Hill.

faqs.question: What are home prices in Fairfield, CT in 2025?
faqs.answer: The median single-family home price in Fairfield is approximately $800,000 as of late 2025, up roughly 6% year over year. Neighborhood pricing varies widely: Greenfield Hill averages over $1.6 million, Southport exceeds $1 million, and beach-area new construction reaches $1.5 to $2 million. More affordable options exist in Stratfield and Fairfield Woods, where renovated colonials list under $700,000. Homes average about 30 days on market, and well-priced properties in the $600,000 to $1 million range frequently receive multiple offers.

faqs.question: How are the public schools in Fairfield ranked?
faqs.answer: Fairfield operates two public high schools that consistently rank among Connecticut's best. Fairfield Warde High School is ranked 11th in the state by Niche and carries an A+ overall rating. Fairfield Ludlowe High School places in the top 10% statewide with a 95% graduation rate, above the Connecticut average of 89%. The private option is Fairfield College Preparatory School, a Jesuit institution on the Fairfield University campus offering 17 AP courses to approximately 800 students.

faqs.question: What beaches and outdoor recreation does Fairfield offer?
faqs.answer: Fairfield maintains five miles of coastline and 170 acres of parkland. Jennings Beach is the largest facility at 27 acres, with lifeguard service, concessions, volleyball, and sailboat storage. Penfield Beach offers pavilion facilities and the Jessica Rekos Memorial Playground. Southport Beach provides more secluded waterfront access near the historic district. Inland, Lake Mohegan has freshwater swimming, a children's sprinkler park, and hiking trails. Dogs and horses are permitted on Jennings Beach from October through March. The town also operates multiple marinas and two golf courses.
